title: Renewable Energy Engineer
description: A Renewable Energy Engineer in India designs, develops, and implements sustainable energy systems, such as solar and wind power plants, and energy-efficient technologies. They work with government agencies, private companies, and research institutions to ensure that energy production is environmentally friendly and meets the country's growing energy demands. In a typical day, they analyze data, conduct site visits, and collaborate with teams to optimize energy production and reduce carbon emissions.
degree_name: Bachelor of Technology in Renewable Energy Engineering
top_private_colleges: Sri Sivasubramaniya Nadar College of Engineering (SSN)

career_progression.year_0_2: Assist in design and development of renewable energy systems, conduct site visits, and participate in team meetings.
career_progression.year_3_5: Take lead in designing and implementing renewable energy projects, conduct feasibility studies, and collaborate with stakeholders.
career_progression.year_6_10: Oversee the development and implementation of large-scale renewable energy projects, lead teams, and mentor junior engineers.
career_progression.entrepreneurship_exit: Start a company that specializes in renewable energy solutions, such as solar panel installation or wind turbine maintenance.
career_progression.global_pathway: Work with international companies or organizations that focus on renewable energy, such as the International Renewable Energy Agency (IRENA) or the World Bank's Energy Sector Management Assistance Program (ESMAP)

india_social_context.first_year_reality: Starting salary is around ₹4-6 lakhs per annum, and working hours can be long, especially during project deadlines.
common_misconceptions: Renewable energy engineers only work on solar panels.
Renewable energy engineers have to work outdoors all the time.
